note: prerequisites are HEURISTIC candidates from body references; curate before use.

items.name: Alignment
items.description: Making a model's behavior match user intent and human values — in the current sources, making language models **helpful, honest, and harmless** rather than merely fluent or large.

items.name: Attention Mechanism
items.description: An attention mechanism maps a query and a set of key–value pairs to an output computed as a weighted sum of the values, where each weight reflects the compatibility of the query with the corresponding key. It lets a model focus on relevant parts of the input regardless of distance.

items.name: Autoregressive Language Model
items.description: A language model that generates a sequence one token at a time, each token conditioned only on the previously generated (left-context) tokens. This is the modeling style of GPT-3 and the decoder side of the Transformer.

items.name: BERT
items.description: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers, 2018) is an **encoder-only** Transformer pre-trained to produce deeply bidirectional language representations, then fine-tuned for downstream tasks. It set new state-of-the-art on eleven NLP tasks.

items.name: Chinchilla
items.description: Chinchilla (*Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models*, DeepMind, 2022) is a 70B-parameter model trained on 4× more data than Gopher under the same compute budget. It outperforms much larger models — including the 175B GPT-3 — establishing that most large LMs were **undertrained**.

items.name: Compute-Optimal Scaling
items.description: The principle, established by Chinchilla (Hoffmann et al., 2022), that under a fixed compute budget a transformer language model's parameter count and number of training tokens should be scaled **equally** — for every doubling of model size, double the training data.

items.name: Emergent Capabilities
items.description: Abilities that appear in large models but are absent (or much weaker) in smaller ones, arising implicitly from Scale and Scaling rather than being explicitly trained.

items.name: Encoder-Decoder Architecture
items.description: The structure the Transformer follows: an encoder maps an input sequence to continuous representations, and a decoder generates an output sequence one element at a time, consuming previously generated symbols (Autoregressive Language Model behavior).

items.name: Few-Shot Learning
items.description: Performing a task from only a few examples. In the GPT-3 context, few-shot means providing a handful of demonstrations in the prompt via In-Context Learning, rather than training on a large labeled dataset.

items.name: Foundation Models
items.description: Coined by Stanford's CRFM (*On the Opportunities and Risks of Foundation Models*, 2021): a model trained on broad data at scale and adaptable to a wide range of downstream tasks. Examples given include BERT, DALL-E, and GPT-3.

items.name: GPT-3
items.description: GPT-3 (*Language Models are Few-Shot Learners*, 2020) is a 175-billion-parameter **decoder-only** Autoregressive Language Model. Its central claim: scaling up language models yields strong task-agnostic Few-Shot Learning via In-Context Learning, often without any fine-tuning.

items.name: Homogenization
items.description: The tendency, described in *Foundation Models*, for a single model to become the shared basis for many downstream applications — concentrating both capability and risk.

items.name: In-Context Learning
items.description: The ability, highlighted by GPT-3, to perform a new task by conditioning on instructions and/or a few examples provided in the prompt at inference time — with **no gradient updates or fine-tuning**.

items.name: Layer Normalization
items.description: A normalization technique applied around each sub-layer in the Transformer, combined with residual connections: the output of each sub-layer is `LayerNorm(x + Sublayer(x))`.

items.name: Masked Language Modeling
items.description: The pre-training objective introduced by BERT: randomly mask ~15% of input tokens and train the model to predict them from surrounding context on both sides.

items.name: Multi-Head Attention
items.description: Instead of a single attention function, the Transformer linearly projects queries, keys, and values h times into lower-dimensional subspaces, applies Scaled Dot-Product Attention in parallel on each, then concatenates and projects the results.

items.name: Next Sentence Prediction
items.description: A secondary pre-training objective in BERT: given two sentences A and B, predict whether B actually follows A in the corpus. Intended to teach inter-sentence relationships useful for tasks like question answering and natural language inference.

items.name: Positional Encoding
items.description: Because the Transformer has no recurrence or convolution, it must inject information about token order explicitly. Positional encodings — added to the input embeddings — provide this.

items.name: Pre-training and Fine-tuning
items.description: A two-stage transfer-learning recipe: first pre-train a model on large unlabeled text with a self-supervised objective, then fine-tune it (with gradient updates) on a smaller labeled dataset for a specific task.

items.name: Proximal Policy Optimization
items.description: PPO is the reinforcement-learning algorithm used in the RL stage of RLHF to fine-tune the language model policy against the learned reward signal from Reward Modeling.

items.name: RLHF
items.description: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback: the technique in *Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback* (2022) used to align GPT-3 into **InstructGPT**. It fine-tunes a model to follow user intent using human preference data.

items.name: Reward Modeling
items.description: The step in RLHF where a model is trained to predict human preferences: given multiple model outputs for a prompt, it learns to score them consistent with human rankings, providing the reward signal for RL.

items.name: Scale and Scaling
items.description: The observation that increasing model size, data, and compute reliably improves language-model capability — a central theme connecting GPT-3 and Foundation Models.

items.name: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
items.description: The specific Attention Mechanism used in the Transformer: `Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax(QKᵀ / √dₖ) V`. Dot products of queries and keys are scaled by 1/√dₖ before the softmax.

items.name: Self-Attention
items.description: Self-attention (intra-attention) is an Attention Mechanism where the queries, keys, and values all come from the same sequence, letting each position attend to every other position to build a context-aware representation.

items.name: Transformer
items.description: The Transformer is a neural network architecture based entirely on Attention Mechanism, dispensing with recurrence and convolution. Introduced in *Attention Is All You Need* (2017), it became the foundation for essentially all modern large language models.
